rationale: The cited paper is used as a key behavioral precedent for the overestimation-based explanation of flat-rate bias, not just as background. The citing paper explicitly summarizes Nunes's ratio-based logic, adapts its Survey 1 choice-task design from Nunes (2000), and tests whether the same usage-estimation mechanism predicts flat-rate choice. It then broadens that line by evaluating overestimation alongside insurance and taxi-meter effects and by validating with matched survey-transaction data in Internet tariffs.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Nunes (2000)'s cognitive overestimation account of flat-rate bias by reusing its core usage-estimation setup and testing its ratio-style prediction. It extends this work to a broader multi-cause framework and real-world tariff-choice data.
